summary: DeVault reflects on a FOSDEM talk by Microsoft/GitHub representatives about open-source sustainability, challenging their fundamental premise. Rather than asking how to manage thousands of dependencies, he argues developers should question why they have thousands of dependencies at all. He contends that maintaining personal relationships with dependency maintainers is the real solution to sustainability crises like left-pad, event-stream, and heartbleed. The post advocates for treating open-source as a community of human relationships rather than a supply chain of black-box components.
key_points: The framing of open-source sustainability as a prioritization problem among thousands of dependencies reveals a fundamentally broken approach to software development
DeVault can list his entire dependency graphs from memory, including maintainers and contributors, and considers depending on unknown libraries maintained by strangers 'absolutely nuts'
Personal relationships with maintainers serve as an early warning system for burnout, abandonment, and security risks that no automated tooling can replace
Crises like left-pad, event-stream, and heartbleed are symptoms of treating dependencies as black boxes rather than community participation
money_quotes: Why do you have thousands of dependencies in the first place?
For most of my projects, I can probably list the entire dependency graph, including transitive dependencies, off of the top of my head.

The idea of depending on a library I've never heard of, several degrees removed via transitive dependencies, maintained by someone I've never met and have no intention of speaking to, is absolutely nuts to me.
Quit treating open-source projects like a black box that conveniently solves your problem.
Engage with the human beings who work on it, participate in the community, and make it healthy and sustainable.
You shouldn't be surprised when the 3 AM alarm goes off if the most you see of a project is a line in your package.json.
If any Node developers are reading, scan through your nearest node_modules directory and see how many of these dependencies you've even heard of.
key_insight: Open-source sustainability isn't a tooling or funding problem — it's a relationship problem caused by developers treating dependencies as interchangeable black boxes instead of engaging with the humans behind them.
